content: Just as vocabulary acquisition for the English word 'reflexion' requires repeated exposure, active recall and contextual reinforcement from high school language curricula, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to incorporate spaced, curriculum-aligned self-reflection practice to improve long-term retention of correct reasoning patterns. This framing treats a model's self-reflection ability not as an innate module but as a learnable skill that needs structured pedagogical practice, just like human vocabulary learning.
